questions.q: In which year was the Vijayanagara Empire founded?
questions.explanation: The Vijayanagara Empire was founded in 1336 CE on the southern bank of the Tungabhadra river.

questions.q: What was the capital city of the Vijayanagara Empire?
questions.explanation: Hampi, on the Tungabhadra river, was the magnificent capital of the Vijayanagara Empire.

questions.q: The name 'Vijayanagara' means which of the following?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara translates as the 'City of Victory'.

questions.q: On the banks of which river was the Vijayanagara capital situated?
questions.explanation: The capital Hampi stood on the southern bank of the Tungabhadra river.

questions.q: Which decisive 1565 battle led to the empire's downfall?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara was crushingly defeated at the Battle of Talikota in 1565.

questions.q: The Vijayanagara Empire was a great centre of which religious tradition?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara was renowned as a great centre of Hindu culture, temple-building, and learning.

questions.q: In which part of India did the Vijayanagara Empire hold sway?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara dominated peninsular southern India for over two centuries.

questions.q: For roughly how long did the Vijayanagara Empire dominate the south?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara dominated peninsular India for more than two centuries after its founding in 1336.

questions.q: The ruins of the Vijayanagara capital are today recognised as a what?
options: A national park with no monuments
questions.explanation: The ruins at Hampi are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

questions.q: Which Muslim Deccan kingdom was Vijayanagara's main early rival?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara's principal early rival was the neighbouring Bahmani Sultanate to its north.

questions.q: Which two brothers are traditionally credited with founding Vijayanagara?
questions.explanation: The brothers Harihara and Bukka founded the empire and its first ruling line in 1336.

questions.q: What was the name of the first ruling dynasty of Vijayanagara?
questions.explanation: The Sangama dynasty, named after the founders' father, was the first of four dynasties to rule Vijayanagara.

questions.q: Which dynasty produced Vijayanagara's most famous ruler, Krishnadevaraya?
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya belonged to the Tuluva dynasty, the third of the four Vijayanagara dynasties.

questions.q: Which alliance of states defeated Vijayanagara at Talikota in 1565?
questions.explanation: An alliance of the Deccan sultanates (the Bahmani successor states) combined to defeat Vijayanagara at Talikota.

questions.q: What happened to the capital Hampi after the defeat at Talikota?
options: It was sacked and largely destroyed
questions.explanation: After Talikota the victorious armies sacked Hampi, and the city never recovered its former glory.

questions.q: Which Italian traveller left a vivid account of the prosperous Vijayanagara capital?
questions.explanation: The Venetian traveller Niccolo de' Conti visited Vijayanagara in the 15th century and described its size and wealth.

questions.q: Which Portuguese traveller described Vijayanagara as a city as large as Rome during Krishnadevaraya's reign?
questions.explanation: The Portuguese chronicler Domingo Paes left a celebrated account of Vijayanagara's grandeur in the early 16th century.

questions.q: Vijayanagara is especially celebrated for its monumental architecture in which form?
options: Stone Hindu temples with towering gopurams
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara is famed for its grand stone temples, ornate pillared halls, and soaring gopuram gateways.

questions.q: The Vijayanagara economy grew rich largely from trade in which valuable commodity, among others?
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara grew wealthy through a flourishing trade in textiles, spices, gems, and the import of war horses.

questions.q: Vijayanagara was administratively divided into provinces governed by chiefs holding land under which system?
questions.explanation: The Nayankara system granted territories to military governors called nayakas in return for service and tribute.

questions.q: How many successive dynasties ruled the Vijayanagara Empire over its history?
questions.explanation: Four dynasties ruled Vijayanagara: Sangama, Saluva, Tuluva, and Aravidu.

questions.q: After Hampi's destruction, the remnant Vijayanagara state under the last dynasty shifted its base to which city?
questions.explanation: The Aravidu dynasty ruled a shrunken successor state from Penukonda and later Chandragiri and Vellore after 1565.

questions.q: Who is traditionally said to have spiritually inspired and guided the founding of Vijayanagara?
questions.explanation: Tradition credits the sage Vidyaranya of Sringeri with inspiring Harihara and Bukka to found the empire.

questions.q: What was the name of the powerful regent and de facto ruler of the Tuluva line before Krishnadevaraya, who founded the dynasty?
options: Tuluva Narasa Nayaka / his son Vira Narasimha
questions.explanation: The Tuluva dynasty was established by Tuluva Narasa Nayaka and his son Vira Narasimha Raya, who preceded Krishnadevaraya.

questions.q: The Saluva dynasty came to power in Vijayanagara through what means?
options: A usurpation by Saluva Narasimha ending Sangama rule
questions.explanation: Saluva Narasimha seized the throne from the declining Sangama line, founding the short-lived Saluva dynasty.

questions.q: The Vittala temple at Hampi is famous for which extraordinary architectural feature?
options: Musical (resonating) stone pillars and a stone chariot
questions.explanation: The Vittala temple complex is renowned for its musical pillars that resonate when struck and its iconic stone chariot.

questions.q: The richly fertile region disputed between Vijayanagara and the Bahmanis was known as the:
questions.explanation: The Raichur doab, between the Krishna and Tungabhadra rivers, was repeatedly contested by Vijayanagara and the Bahmanis.

questions.q: Krishnadevaraya's Telugu work on statecraft and kingship is titled:
questions.explanation: Krishnadevaraya authored the Telugu poem Amuktamalyada, which includes reflections on governance.

questions.q: Which last effective regent of Vijayanagara commanded its forces at Talikota and was killed there?
questions.explanation: Aliya Rama Raya, the powerful regent of the puppet king Sadasiva, led the army at Talikota and was captured and executed.

questions.q: The Vijayanagara rulers gave special prominence to the worship of which deity, reflected in many capital-city temples?
options: Virupaksha (a form of Shiva) and Vishnu/Krishna
questions.explanation: Vijayanagara kings were great patrons of Shaiva (Virupaksha) and Vaishnava worship, building major temples to both at the capital.
